context: Tests whether the agent applies the thermo-nuclear review skill rigorously: it must identify all four seeded structural problems (misleading name, shallow interface with passthrough methods, multi-concern god-function, and the coupon conditional chain), propose concrete code-judo simplifications that delete complexity rather than rearrange it, and avoid false-positive nits on cosmetic details.

checklist.name: Identifies all meaningful issues
checklist.description: The agent flags all four seeded problems: (1) class name contradicts its behavior, (2) passthrough getters expose internals, (3) validateAndSubmit does validation + pricing + persistence + notification, (4) the coupon/tax/shipping conditional chain. Award partial credit if 3 of 4 are caught.

checklist.name: Code-judo on the conditional chain
checklist.description: For the coupon/tax/shipping conditionals, the agent proposes replacing the if-else chain with a data-driven approach (e.g. a map or array of adjustment functions) that deletes the branching entirely — not just extracting it into a helper with the same structure.

checklist.name: Names anti-patterns correctly
checklist.description: The agent uses the skill's vocabulary: at minimum calls out 'shallow interface' for the passthrough getters, 'spaghetti growth' or equivalent for the conditional chain, and 'wrong-layer logic' or 'mixed concerns' for the god-function.

checklist.name: Proposes specific, minimal refactors
checklist.description: Each proposed fix is concrete — e.g. shows a renamed class, a specific interface change, or a skeleton of the adjustment-map pattern — rather than vague advice like 'split this up' or 'consider a better name'.

checklist.name: Distinguishes shallow from deep interfaces
checklist.description: The agent explicitly distinguishes the passthrough getDb/getMailer/getAuditLog methods as a shallow-interface smell (callers must still know internal structure) and proposes a deeper interface where those concerns are hidden.

checklist.name: No false-positive cosmetic issues
checklist.description: The agent does not spend significant review weight on cosmetic or naming-only problems (e.g. parameter order, minor variable naming, comment style). The review is focused on structural and abstraction issues.

checklist.name: Produces a structured report
checklist.description: The output is organized — not a wall of prose. Issues are separated and prioritized (structural regressions first, then missed simplifications, then boundary/abstraction problems), matching the skill's output ordering.
